alerts.title: No 5xx API responses during rollout validation
alerts.description: Any server-side API failure during smoke or canary validation blocks rollout.

alerts.title: P95 request latency stays within the rollout budget
alerts.description: Healthy canary traffic should keep request latency below the 1.5s budget.

alerts.title: No durable jobs dead-letter during rollout validation
alerts.description: Dead-lettered jobs indicate a correctness regression in queue or worker handling.

alerts.title: No worker execution failures during rollout validation
alerts.description: The rollout gate fails if any worker job reports a failed execution.

alerts.title: Cockpit first meaningful render stays inside the rollout budget
alerts.description: The redesigned cockpit must become meaningfully visible within 2.5s during canary validation.

alerts.title: Cockpit summary assembly stays inside the rollout budget
alerts.description: Dashboard summary assembly must stay under the canary latency budget.

alerts.title: Cockpit table endpoints stay inside the rollout budget
alerts.description: Goal, approval, memory, and operations table refreshes must remain responsive during canary validation.

alerts.title: Cockpit event reconnects stay bounded
alerts.description: Excessive reconnects indicate the cockpit cannot keep live state stable.

alerts.title: Cockpit approval latency stays inside the rollout budget
alerts.description: Approval decisions should remain inside the ten-minute operating-loop budget during rollout.

alerts.title: Cockpit dead-letter recovery stays inside the rollout budget
alerts.description: Dead-letter replay should start within the fifteen-minute recovery objective.

alerts.title: Integration calls stay error-free during rollout validation
alerts.description: Provider failures are surfaced immediately, but they are advisory when the rollout smoke path does not exercise live integrations.

alerts.title: Telemetry exporter stays healthy while retention is enabled
alerts.description: Backend export failures should page operators even when retained batches keep telemetry durable.

alerts.title: Recommendation safe precision stays above the quality floor
alerts.description: If replay-backed recommendation precision decays, operators should pause promotion and inspect the learning set before autonomy widens further.

alerts.title: Recommendation safe recall proxy stays above the discovery floor
alerts.description: A collapsing recall proxy means safe reusable patterns are no longer being surfaced consistently, even if the remaining suggestions are precise.

alerts.title: Recommendation negative outcome rate stays inside the guardrail
alerts.description: High negative outcome rates indicate drift or false-positive recommendation behavior that should keep learning signals out of live autonomy.

alerts.title: Recommendation failure cost stays bounded
alerts.description: Failure-cost spikes mean the wrong learning signal is expensive enough to require rollback or promotion disablement.

alerts.title: Rejected or overridden recommendations do not spike
alerts.description: A spike in rejected or overridden recommendations is an operator-visible signal that recommendation quality has decayed and promotion should be reviewed.
